title: Opening Command sent too soon during SSH interactive login
author: barcoboy
body_snippet: When trying to login to an SSH connection requiring an interactive login and no stored credentials are specified in the connection profile, if a command is specified in the Opening Command field, it is entered as the username of the login prompt, or if only a username is stored in the profile, the command is entered as the first password.  Is it possible to wait until credentials are satisfied for a user's interactive login before sending the Opening Command text?  If both a username and passwor...

iterations.description: Opening command is sent before the SSH shell is ready during interactive login, causing commands to be dropped or mishandled — a timing/race condition bug.
Bulk verified: commit ee3a7354 exists (HIGH confidence)

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

comments.snippet:
Unfortunately still not working.  If no credentials are saved, the login proceeds after manually entering in credentials, but the opening command is not entered in afterwards, even when increasing the "Maximum PuTTY and integrated external tools wait time" to a larger value.  If credentials are saved, upon initiating the connection, a dialog box appears that says:

```
PuTTY Command Line Error

option '-batch' not available in this tool
```

However, this is also occurring if there is nothing in...

notes:
AI triage (claude): Opening command is sent before the SSH shell is ready during interactive login, causing commands to be dropped or mishandled — a timing/race condition bug.
Approach: Delay sending the opening command until the SSH shell prompt is detected or a ready signal is received from the channel, rather than sending immediately after connection establishment.
